scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. {{SUIT_NUMBER}}/2024

BETWEEN:

RAM KUMAR
Age: 45 years
Resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru

... PLAINTIFF

AND:

SURESH PATEL
Age: 38 years
Resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru

... DEFENDANT

PLAINT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY

The Plaintiff above named submits as follows:

JURISDICTION

1. This Court has jurisdiction to try this suit under Section 9 and Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

2. The Defendant resides within the jurisdiction of this Court at Koramangala, Bengaluru. The cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Court as the loan was advanced from Bengaluru and the legal notice was issued from Bengaluru.

FACTS OF THE CASE

3. The Plaintiff is a resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, and is competent to file this suit.

4. The Defendant is a resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, and is liable to repay the amount claimed herein.

5. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000 to the Defendant.

6. The said amount of Rs. 15,00,000 was transferred via NEFT bank transfer bearing UTR: AXIB20240315123456 dated 15.03.2024.

7. There was no written agreement executed between the Plaintiff and the Defendant regarding the said loan.

8. The Defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages.

9. The Defendant failed to repay the loan amount. The date of default for interest calculation is 20.06.2024.

10. The Plaintiff made repeated oral demands for repayment.

11. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to the Defendant demanding repayment.

12. Despite the legal notice and demands, the Defendant has failed to repay the amount.

LEGAL BASIS

13. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover the principal amount and interest under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, as the Defendant has committed a breach of the obligation to repay the loan.

14. The Plaintiff is entitled to claim interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, from the date of default until realization.

CAUSE OF ACTION

15. The cause of action arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant defaulted on the repayment.

16. The cause of action is continuing as the money remains unpaid despite demands and legal notice dated 01.07.2024.

LIMITATION

17. The suit is within limitation as per Article 19 of the Limitation Act, 1963, which prescribes a period of three years from the date when the loan is made. The loan was made on 15.03.2024, and the suit is filed within the prescribed period.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

18. The suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000 for the purpose of court fee and jurisdiction.

19. The Plaintiff pays a court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} as per the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

PRAYER

20. The Plaintiff prays for judgment and decree against the Defendant for:

(a) Recovery of principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000;
(b) Interest at 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 until realization;
(c) Costs of the suit; and
(d) Any other relief this Court deems fit to grant.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

1. The Plaintiff relies upon the following documents:

(a) NEFT bank transfer record with UTR: AXIB20240315123456 dated 15.03.2024;
(b) WhatsApp messages acknowledging the loan;
(c) Legal notice dated 01.07.2024.

VERIFICATION

1. I, RAM KUMAR, the Plaintiff herein, do hereby verify and declare that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 20 of the plaint are true and correct to my knowledge and belief, and no part of it is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at Bengaluru on this {{DATE}} day of {{MONTH}}, 2024.

PLAINTIFF

ADVOCATE FOR PLAINTIFF
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
{{ADVOCATE_CONTACT}}
Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No. {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}

citation_issues.message: 'Section 20 the Code' not in verified_provisions — may be correct but unverified
'Section 20' not in verified_provisions — may be correct but unverified
'Section 73 Indian Contract' not in verified_provisions — may be correct but unverified
